title: The Pros & Cons of The University of Michigan | Honest Review of Campus Life, Academics, and More!
body:
I attended Vassar College for undergrad, and Michigan for grad school. When I got to Michigan I was so blown away at how much the university and Ann Arbor had to offer --- especially if I had been an undergraduate attending the school --- that I wished I had gone to Michigan for undergrad.

Also, I grew up 15 miles from Cambridge, MA, and Harvard and MIT psychologically dominate the Greater Boston area in terms of academic perception. So I spent a lot of time in Cambridge as a youth, exposed to Harvard and MIT students, and meeting people outside of Cambridge who had attended Harvard. What struck me about Michigan is that the students were just as brilliant, just as accomplished, just as high-achieving ---but they lacked the myopic and delusional self-aggrandizement that Harvard students and graduates possessed.

Also, if you attend Michigan, and you're from outside of the state, you MUST visit the eastern shore of Lake Michigan, cross the Mackinac Bridge into the Upper Peninsula, and visit the shore of Lake Superior, getting to Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore. Those lakes are actually MORE beautiful than the coast of Maine, and you're really missing something if you spend 4 years in Ann Arbor and don't visit them.
author: @ToMakeWhatLifesAbout

title: The Pros & Cons of The University of Michigan | Honest Review of Campus Life, Academics, and More!

I just visited Ann Arbor today for fun, and it was so cute like I can’t even. 😅 I’m currently a sophomore in high school and I’m looking at where to go to college. The grade thing kinda worries me tho, with only a certain amount of A’s/B’s to give out. And the dorm situation. But still a cool college. 💙💛

author: @ShreyaMalhotra-m6h

title: The Pros & Cons of The University of Michigan | Honest Review of Campus Life, Academics, and More!
body:
Hello Josie, Imposter syndrome is not a "con" for UMich. Yes it exists and btw way you are not an average student. Let's think about "average" for a minute. National average for ACT is about 20 with standard deviation of 5,8, so the average ACT is 14.2 to 25.8, which covers 68% of students who take the ACT. 95% (+/- 2 sigma 2x5.8=31.8 score)) covers 95% of test takers. The average ACT for Umich admitted student is 31 to 34. (admittedly some students get in with slightly lower scores, so let's say some score in the 90th percentile which is a 28 on the ACT). So it is safe to say Umich students are not "average"- they likely are in the top 10% of all students. Looking at that 10% the average for the" UMICH admitted group" is 95th percentile which translates to 30 to 31 ACT score. So it may be true you could possibly be "average" amongst Umich students, but certainly you are shortchanging yourself thinking you are an average student. Never underestimate yourself. The imposter syndrome is ubiquitous amongst all of the elite schools, so it's not accurate to say it is a con for UofM. It's part of student life and a stressor for sure, but this syndrome shouldn't be called a con for Umich.  

FYI- I earned 2 degrees from Michigan; BS mathematics(1967) and MBA finance(1977). My granddaughter just graduated May 3, 2025 with a BSE in chemical engineering with a GPA of 3.9, so she may have been one of those competitive students. Ross is an extraordinarily competitive environment even for Michigan. (What else can I say, Ross is Ross.) I can assure you the students in the CoE studying chemical engineering are not cutthroat competitive. They obviously want to be the best they can be and shoot for the top, but they understand collaboration gives better results for everyone involved. FYI the mathematics depart in LS&A is ranked in the top 10 in THE WORLD and they put out a bulletin to entering g first years that they can not pass calculus unless they collaborate-interesting dichotomy isn't it? "Competition" is a feature of American society and is even more prevalent when functioning near the top of the population like you do. There's an old adage, "To whom much is given, much is expected" so you are blessed to be functioning in a highly competitive arena. Michigan is what it is because the expectations for its students and alumni are to make the world a better place because you have the ability to do that.

BTW, Josie, Ann Arbor is NOT ranked as "ONE of the best college towns". It is consistently ranked THE BEST college town. 😊

You did a great job explaining your experience and perspective on The University of Michigan. I enjoyed your thoughtfulness and research and you are an outstanding speaker/presenter. You will do well in the business world. Remember you will always be proud to be a Michigan Wolverine. Again, never underestimate yourself. Like I told my chemical engineer granddaughter, You may not be the smartest one in the room, but you can always be the hardest working. She proved that over the past 4 years. GO BLUE!
author: @williamsturley6631

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: None of the psych wards in my area allow a cell phones and very limited/monitored computer time. Phone calls are monitored and only allowed for a few minutes per day regardless of your mental status.
author: @workingman-xl6xh

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It always shocks me how different psych wards can be in different places. I was admitted to a psychiatric institution involuntarily and was there for 2 weeks. I had a roommate, we weren't allowed outside of our room from 9pm-7am and weren't allowed back in our room from 7am-7pm. Eating breakfast, lunch, and dinner was mandatory and we all had to be present in the cafeteria, no one was allowed to stay back. We had to attend group sessions to talk about battling mental illness and coping mechanisms. It wasn't very effective. We had a TV in the common area, but they wouldn't turn it on unless it was to watch a movie. I spent all of my free time reading and coloring. We weren't allowed to go outside and only had one window in the whole ward. It literally felt like prison.
author: @halidyer5595

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
Can I say that seeing how her partner supports her during this challenging time is more comforting than anything else? As a person who is severely mentally ill, seeing that there is still hope in finding a life-long partner that will accept me for who I am is more impactful than anything I could ever find on the internet.
Thank you, Lauren, for sharing your authentic self online. It really helps those who are struggling to feel seen and understood.
author: @paigeruff5752

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
When I was in my late 20s I admitted myself into a psych hospital for suicidal thoughts and because I was experiencing things in my life that I did not know how to handle.
At the time, the hospital was sort of a safe haven for me. At the time it’s what I needed for support of what I was going through.
Sometimes people just need that hospital for things they’re going through.
Wishing you the best.
author: @crystalclear6660

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in the psych ward after trying to unsubscribe from life. It help somewhat but they also told me my current doctor was prescribing meds that interact and way too high a dose… sending you ❤
author: @kristenkirk6108

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Last week I went to the ER and someone was having a some sort of psychiatric break. She was clearly terrified and screaming. Seeing the way staff treated her was heartbreaking. I kept hearing her screaming “get off of me” and hearing the nurses giggling about “the pipes on that chick” and “it’s so and sos favorite patient”. It was very disheartening.

title: How I Got Expelled From Western Michigan University
body:
Dude, it is almost criminal that your videos don't get more views and you don't have more subscribers. They are really well edited. YouTube's algo isn't picking them up for some reason.... likely because of consistency but I know the catch 22. Takes a lot of time and footage from multiple Cameras to tell the full story and takes tons of editing. Exhausting to say the least. I have ran into this same situation on my channels over the years. Sometimes the thought of editing all the footage makes me sick and I can never bring myself to do it.
I don't know what the answer is for you right now because you should probably finish college and at any point if you focused fully on videos it gets frustrating if they aren't gaining traction like they should.
I don't know what the answer is man. I do wish you luck. You have a lot of talent

title: How I Got Expelled From Western Michigan University
body: Western Michigan University is a doctoral research university with many rigorous programs. You are expected to study, do research, learn and not party in the process.. Even the foul language in your video demonstrates that you aren’t ready for college. I do hope that you return to some college and earn a degree. Use your potential that will impact you and those around you.

title: An Aviation Student's Campus Tour
body: Very nice video! Just retired with Honeywell Aerospace in Phoenix Arizona. Although I majored in flight at Western in the 70’s, I worked in engineering customer support for Honeywell products working with all airlines in the world and have traveled to them.  Keep your horizons and dreams open!!  GO BRONCOS!!!

title: Western Michigan University // A walk around campus
body: Thank you for the video.  I also graduated from WMU xx years ago, but haven't been able to get back to Kalamazoo in years since I now live across the country.  Some things on campus have changed, but I see a lot of things look the same as when I was there.  Good memories of that campus...
